attempted_route: pd.read_json(TRAIN_PATH, orient='split', compression='gzip') + MODData.featurize(fast=False, n_jobs=1) + MODNetModel(num_neurons=([256],[128],[16],[16]), act='elu') + model.fit/predict
observation: Crashed at line 15 after 3.5 s: gzip.BadGzipFile 'Not a gzipped file (b'\x80\x05')'; dataset is pickle protocol 5, not gzip-JSON
rationale: The agent assumed matbench files are gzip-JSON, but the actual on-disk format is Python pickle (magic bytes 0x80 0x05 = pickle protocol 5). The correct loader is pd.read_pickle(), not pd.read_json().
recommended_alternative: Replace pd.read_json(path, orient='split', compression='gzip') with pd.read_pickle(path) for both train and test. Keep all MODData/MODNetModel calls unchanged: MODData(materials=df['structure'].tolist(), targets=df[['gap_expt_eV']], target_names=['gap_expt_eV']), featurize(n_jobs=1), feature_selection(n=150), MODNetModel([[target]], num_neurons=([256],[128],[16],[16]), act='elu', n_feat=150).
